heading: Limitations on authority
label: 31147 Limitations on authority

L. 98–554, § 22998 Stat. 285331147(b)49 App.:2519(b).In subsection (a), the word “prescribe” is substituted for “establish or maintain” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.

children.heading: .—Traffic Regulations
children.label: a .—Traffic Regulations
children.text: This subchapter does not authorize the Secretary of Transportation to prescribe traffic safety regulations or preempt State traffic regulations. However, the Secretary may prescribe traffic regulations to the extent their subject matter was regulated under parts 390–399 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations, on .October 30, 1984

children.heading: .—Regulating the Manufacturing of Vehicles
children.label: b .—Regulating the Manufacturing of Vehicles
children.text: This subchapter does not authorize the Secretary to regulate the manufacture of commercial motor vehicles for any purpose, including fuel economy, safety, or emission control.
